footer.tagline: Slack-native access management. Ask, approve, audit — without leaving the chat.

hero.statusPillDesktop: Slack-first access management · First month free
hero.subheadline: Hand off access, keep the visibility.
hero.lede: Tool owners approve in Slack. You see who has what, when you want.
hero.finePrint: First month free · Card required · Slack admin install only

standout1.titleLine1: "What tools does the new hire need?"
standout1.bodyAfter: to see Sarah's tools as checkboxes. Tick the ones you want, hit Request. Owners get pinged for each tool — one click each to approve.
bullets: See your teammate's shelf as a checklist
Batch the request, owners review one DM each
Auto-approved tools skip the queue entirely

standout2.body: Assign one or more owners to each tool from the dashboard. When someone asks for Figma, the head of design gets the DM — not the CTO. Multi-owner committees supported; first click resolves the request, the others' DMs auto-update.
bullets: Multi-owner fan-out — any one resolves it
Admins are a fallback, not the default

standout3.bodyBefore: Most access tools are web apps that send Slack pings. Cookie Jar is a Slack app that has a web dashboard. Type
standout3.bodyAfter: and the full command surface is right there — request, list, configure, audit. The dashboard is for monthly review, not daily ops.
bullets: Zero new UI for employees to learn
Approve in Slack. Audit on the dashboard.
CTO opens the dashboard maybe once a month

realSlackOutput.lede: No stylized renders. These are the ephemeral messages Cookie Jar posts back when you run a command — same copy, same structure.

demos.desc: When a tool isn't on the shelf, Cookie Jar suggests the closest match and lists the full shelf.
Your jar. Every tool you have, who granted it, in one ephemeral list.
Anyone in your workspace can peek at a teammate's jar — basis for /cookie like.
Admin view of every cookie on the shelf, its approval policy, and the owner if there is one.

items.title: One-click approve / deny
items.body: Owners get a Slack DM with [Approve] [Deny] buttons. One click resolves the request, the audit log captures the decision, and the requester gets DM'd the outcome.

items.title: /cookie like {'@'}teammate
items.body: Clone a teammate's tool list in one command. Type /cookie like {'@'}sarah, see her tools as checkboxes, tick what the new hire needs, hit Request. Owners get pinged for each tool — one click each to approve.

items.title: 24 tools pre-loaded
items.body: Jira, GitHub, Figma, Notion, Linear, AWS, GCP, Datadog, Sentry, Stripe, and 14 more. Plus your own custom tools — up to 3 on Starter, up to 20 on Pro, unlimited on Family Size.

items.title: Real-time access overview
items.body: Dashboard view of who has what across the org. Person cards with tool badges. Filter, search, see at a glance.

items.title: Audit log on every action
items.body: Every grant, revoke, approve, deny, admin promotion — recorded with actor, target, tool, and timestamp. Browseable view in Slack and dashboard coming soon.

items.title: Onboarding DM for new joiners
items.body: When someone joins your Slack workspace, Cookie Jar auto-DMs them with a starter command list. They self-serve from minute one.

offboarding.titleLine2: empty the jar in one command.
offboarding.bodyAfter: and Cookie Jar will list every tool Bob has, who owns each one, and what it costs you per month — and DM each owner a revoke checklist. No Notion page, no spreadsheet, no Friday-evening guesswork.
bullets: Complete picture of someone's access in one place
Owners get a per-tool revoke checklist in Slack
Cost-per-seat surfaced so you know what you save
offboarding.honestyBefore: Not yet built. Today, offboarding is manual — you check the dashboard for who has what and ask each owner to revoke (same shape as adding a seat). The
offboarding.honestyAfter: auto-flow ships on Pro alongside auto-provisioning.

steps.title: Types /cookie request github
steps.body: Or /cookie like {'@'}sarah for a batch. Cookie Jar parses the command and validates the tool exists on the shelf.

steps.title: Routes to the tool owner
steps.body: Looks up the owner(s) for that tool. Falls back to admins if none. Owner gets a Slack DM with [Approve] [Deny].

steps.title: Clicks Approve
steps.body: One click in the Slack DM. Multi-owner committees fan out — first click resolves, others auto-update.

steps.title: Adds the seat in GitHub
steps.body: 30-second manual step in the actual tool. Owner is already in the headspace — they just decided yes.

steps.title: Logs grant, DMs requester
steps.body: Audit log captures actor, target, tool, timestamp. Requester gets the outcome. Dashboard updates in real time.

steps.title: Offboarding — manual for now
steps.body: When someone leaves, you check the dashboard for who has what, then ping each owner to revoke. Same shape as adding a seat: Cookie Jar gives you the picture, the actual click is still in the third-party tool.

pipeline.roadmapCalloutBody: Auto-provisioning (step 04) and the full offboarding flow (step 06) ship as part of Pro, via n8n integrations to GitHub, Jira, and Figma first.

problem.lede: At 10 people, you ask in #general. At 30, you DM the founder. At 50, you've built a Notion page no one reads. Cookie Jar is what you use before you need a real IT department.

cards.head: Every access request becomes a CTO DM
cards.body: GitHub, Figma, AWS — they all funnel to the one person who installed the workspace three years ago.
cards.quote: "hey can you add me to figma 🙏"

cards.head: Onboarding takes days, not minutes
cards.body: New hire asks "what tools do I need?" Nobody knows the full list. They get half of it on day one and the rest over the next week.
cards.quote: "wait, am I supposed to have Linear too?"

cards.head: Nobody knows who has what
cards.body: Six months in, you can't name a single person who has GitHub admin. Or AWS root. Or Figma editor.

cards.head: Offboarding is a guessing game
cards.body: Someone leaves on Friday. You spend the weekend hoping you remembered to revoke everything they had.

support.title: Need help?
support.intro: We're a small team. A human reads every message. We respond within {responseTime} — usually faster.

emailCard.title: Email us
emailCard.body: Bug reports, billing questions, feature requests, security concerns — any of it.

fasterAnswers.title: Faster answers
fasterAnswers.body: Most questions are already answered in our FAQ.

serviceStatus.title: Service status
serviceStatus.body: Cookie Jar runs at {url}. If something's broken, email us and we'll dig in.

support.footer: Already a customer? You can also reach us from inside the dashboard's feedback widget.
